mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.26% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS above 5.0% signals continued credit tightening that depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.4% signals a tight labor market near full employment. When UNRATE remains below 4.5%, wage pressures intensify, forcing firms to accept margin compression. This cost-push dynamic reduces near-term earnings growth expectations, driving negative equity returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%) when UNRATE < 4.5%, or correlation between UNRATE and 5-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1525K units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) at $367.5B indicate robust consumer demand. When both HOUST and RSAFS grow month-over-month, this signals sustained aggregate demand that supports equity earnings growth. The joint strength of housing and consumption predicts positive 60-day SPX returns as forward earnings revisions accelerate.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1525K and RSAFS at $367.5B as of 2007-02
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1%) when both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month growth, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 113.14 reflects manufacturing capacity utilization. A 3-month decline in INDPRO below its 6-month moving average signals weakening supply-side momentum and deteriorating production efficiency. This supply contraction predicts negative 20-day equity returns as profit margins compress and growth expectations reset downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines below its 3-month moving average, or Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 137.6M persons combined with rising UNRATE signals a labor market inflection. When PAYEMS growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month while UNRATE rises, this signals weakening labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades. The interaction of slowing job creation and rising unemployment predicts negative 60-day SPX returns as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100K AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137.6M and UNRATE at 4.4% as of 2007-03
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%) when PAYEMS growth < 100K and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 203.91 combined with FEDFUNDS at 5.26% indicates the Fed maintains a real policy rate above neutral. When CPI growth accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, the Fed's restrictive stance persists, signaling continued equity headwinds. This regime predicts negative 20-day returns as rate-cut expectations fade.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.91 and FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of 2007-02-03
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPI YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at $11,513B (last available Q4 2006) combined with RSAFS at $367.5B indicates aggregate demand remains solid. When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS shows month-over-month expansion, this signals sustained economic momentum that supports equity valuations. The mechanism predicts positive 60-day SPX returns as forward earnings estimates remain stable or improve.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at $11,513B as of 2006-Q4 and RSAFS at $367.5B as of 2007-02
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%) when RSAFS shows month-over-month growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 113.14 combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 137.6M reflects supply-side capacity constraints. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while PAYEMS growth remains above 100K, this signals a supply bottleneck where firms cannot expand output despite labor availability. This mismatch predicts positive 5-day SPX returns as inflation expectations rise and equities benefit from pricing power.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 100K
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.14 and PAYEMS at 137.6M as of 2007-02-03
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0%) when INDPRO growth < 0.5% and PAYEMS growth > 100K, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.
